title: CONFLICT payload
description: Emitted by the router (or coordinator) when an agent's INTENTION overlaps with another agent's actively-claimed scope. Routed to the agent whose intention triggered the conflict so that agent can pause, pivot, or coordinate. CONFLICT and BLOCK are intentionally distinct: BLOCK means 'I am stuck and need help'; CONFLICT means 'your action collides with someone else's claim and should not proceed as-is'.

intention_id.description: The msg_id of the INTENTION that triggered this conflict signal.

started_at_ms.description: When the conflicting intention became active. Earlier timestamp generally wins precedence.

conflicting_intentions.description: All currently-active intentions whose scope overlaps with the triggering intention.

kind.description: Type of conflict. 'scope_overlap': scopes intersect under the configured semantics. 'exclusive_claim': another agent declared blocks_others on a scope you are trying to touch. 'policy_block': operator policy forbids this combination. 'dependency_wait': your action depends on another agent's pending resolution.

overlapping_scopes.description: The specific scopes that overlap. Useful for narrowing a pivot.

suggested_resolution.description: Hint from the router/coordinator on how to resolve. Advisory, not enforced.

rationale.description: Free-text explanation, especially useful when the resolution comes from the coordinator (LLM-mediated).
